payload.friendly_name: Slice F — Multi-Context Extensibility + Strategic Remediations
payload.purpose_context: Delivers Issue #1111 Slice F's three architectural seams — organisation-tier doctrine graph, monorepo-aware charter scope, composable workflow sequencing — alongside five strategic remediations surfaced during Mission B's post-merge review. The bundling is deliberate: the remediations (ratchet burn-down, symbol-level dead code, alias clean removal, catalog-miss CLI visibility, contract round-trip backstop) each protect Slice F's quality of execution by closing failure modes Slice F's new modules would otherwise multiply. Auth-transport cleanup is descoped to the lead maintainer per the SaaS auth-caution policy.
payload.purpose_tldr: Open spec-kitty's architecture for organisations, monorepos, and custom workflows while hardening the ratchets that protect future quality.

payload.question: Cat-7 orphans #8 + #9 (specify_cli.glossary.prompts + specify_cli.glossary.rendering) ship as dead code. Disposition for Mission C: WIRE (audit conflict-resolution pipeline + integrate), DELETE (remove the modules + their tests), or KEEP-WITH-RATIONALE (allowlist with a stronger justification + sunset date)?

payload.final_answer: DELETE — remove specify_cli.glossary.prompts and specify_cli.glossary.rendering modules and their tests. Rationale: 3+ years orphaned without operator demand for interactive conflict resolution; cleanest Cat-7 burn-down; mirrors HiC §5a.1 'no eventual deprecation' stance. This contributes 2 entries to the FR-113 Cat-7 shrinkage (from 10 → at most 7), letting the burn-down PR ship with 3 entries removed: doctrine.templates.repository + glossary.prompts + glossary.rendering = 10 → 7.

payload.wp_title: Workflow runtime integration — `spec-kitty next` consumes workflow + back-compat + unknown-id hard-fail
Org-DRG integration — `build_charter_context` wiring + `doctor doctrine` + provenance render
Workflow sequence YAML schema + registry + Pydantic model + `meta.json::workflow_id`
DRIFT-1 alias clean deletion + tests migration + ImportError regression test
CLI logging bootstrap + Rich-aware handler + subprocess visibility test
Org-DRG operator UX — `doctrine org init` + `doctrine org validate` + partial glossary
Contract round-trip CI gate + frontmatter convention + legacy allowlist
Ratchet baseline + meta-test + Cat-7 per-category refactor + Cat-7 shrinkage 10→7
Org-layer DRG — schema, loader, merge, conflict policy, validator extension
Closing — cross-axis integration tests + glossary canonical promotion + READMEs + charter amendments + auth-transport ADR + GitHub ticket
Symbol-level dead-code gate (`__all__` walk) + `__all__` on src/charter/ + src/kernel/

reason: Lane-A foundation: ratchet baselines + per-category refactor + Cat-7 shrinkage 10->7 (deleted doctrine.templates.repository + glossary.{prompts,rendering} per Q5)

T003+T004 merge defensible: meta-test body was complete in T001 RED (307 lines), only data file was missing. Stubs inert (pytestmark skip). 161 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed in tests/architectural/. Ruff clean.

Cycle 2: test assertion tightened to require Rich-format (per cycle-1 reviewer + reframed spec 25066955). Real ATDD red→green now verified. Pre-bootstrap output lacks level prefix → assertion FAILS. With bootstrap → 'WARNING  Charter catalog miss' matches → PASSES.

review.reference: Cycle-2 approved: assertion tightened to require RichHandler's WARNING<2-spaces> format; real ATDD red→green verified (raw Python defaults at pre-bootstrap commit produce no WARNING prefix → assertion correctly fails). Cycle-1 substantive findings (bootstrap mechanics, unit tests, lane purity) carry forward unchanged. WP05's reframed value: deterministic Rich-formatted catalog-miss output (cosmetic UX), not RISK-3 fix (misdiagnosis confirmed).

review.reference: WP06 approved: OrgDRGFragment + loader + merge + charter lint extension; 4 ATDD GREEN (6 tests, red→green verified); round-trip OrgDRGFragment flipped SKIPPED→PASSED on org-drg-schema::block-2 and block-3 (binding criterion met). C-009 reused via local re-declaration — byte-identical to charter.activations._ALLOWED_KINDS (follow-up: consider tests/architectural/test_kind_universe_consistency.py to prevent drift). Layer-rule heuristic is substring-based on body_path/title/id; false-negative risk noted for indirect transitive references (acceptable for v1 — document as known limitation). Provenance sidecar via object.__setattr__ on frozen Pydantic models works reliably; unit-tested at lines 365/387/402/488 of test_org_drg_loader.py for all three source values. WP01-WP05 gates: 17 passed. Pre-existing non-WP06 failures: test_plan_mission_yaml_validates.py (circular import, predates WP06 by mission 1KQ4ARB), test_events_envelope and test_next_* (pre-existing lane failures). NOTE: round-trip ratchet-baseline-format.md::block-1 failure is introduced by the lane-purity-guard cleanup commit (5408e732) not by WP06 substantive code — planning branch has the correct fix; mission-merge will reconcile.
